ogTitle: Stoters Hill vs Strathdickie: Climate Safety

heading: Stoters Hill vs Strathdickie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stoters Hill, Queensland and Strathdickie, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Strathdickie has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Stoters Hill (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stoters Hill or Strathdickie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stoters Hill has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Strathdickie scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stoters Hill scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Strathdickie scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stoters Hill or Strathdickie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Strathdickie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
